figures.caption: Figure 1. Current learning paradigms of modeling WSIs for survival analysis. Unlike previous approaches, the proposed STEPH efficiently utilizes the generalizable prognostic knowledge from other cancers by model merging.
Table 1. Comparison with baselines in WSI prognosis tasks. It reports the mean and standard deviation of C-Index in five-fold crossvalidation. Best in bold, second best underline.
Figure 6. More results of TVM loss landscape.
Figure 9. Dynamics of λ and w in training. This example shows a case where t = BRCA and 12 cancer-specific models (Ms) are merged with Mt by STEPH to obtain M∗ t .

Figure 3. Sparse Task Vector Mixup with Hypernetworks (STEPH) for efficient knowledge transfer in WSI prognosis. After computing task vectors, STEPH first applies mixup to each paired (τt, τs) to absorb prognostic knowledge from cross-cancer models. Then, the most beneficial mixtures are selected and aggregated to derive M∗ t for prediction. Hypernetworks drive these steps by steering task vectors.
